name: Apple Cider Tartlets
recipeInstructions:
1. Allow both crust pouches to stand at room temperature for 15 to 20 minutes.
2. Heat oven to 400 degrees F (200 degrees C).
3. Unfold pie crust and peel off the top plastic sheet.
4. Sprinkle 1 teaspoon flour over the crust. Place the crust, floured side down, on a cutting board or pastry cloth and peel off the remaining plastic sheet.
5. Using a 2-1/2-inch cookie cutter, cut 12 circles from the crust. Repeat with the remaining pie crust and flour.
6. Press the dough circles into and up the sides of 24 ungreased miniature muffin cups until the pastry extends 1/4 inch above each cup; set aside.
7. In a small bowl, combine frozen apple juice concentrate (thawed), brown sugar, cinnamon, melted butter or margarine, vanilla, and egg.
8. Spoon the filling into each pastry-lined cup, filling each about 3/4 full.
9. Bake at 400 degrees F for 17 to 24 minutes, or until the filling is set and the crusts are light golden brown.
10. Cool in the pans for 2 minutes before removing the tartlets.
11. When ready to serve, pipe or spoon whipped cream onto each tartlet.
12. Store in the refrigerator. Makes 24 tartlets.
Source: Classic Pillsbury Cookbooks (Country Baking) Dietary Exchange: 1 Bread, 1 Fat, 130 calories, 7 grams fat, 20 mg cholesterol, 80 mg sodium
